display.purpose: Balance hero — the primary financial number. One instance per screen.

headline.purpose: Stat card values (income, expenses, savings). Big, bold, readable at a glance.

body.purpose: Expense descriptions, form field values, general content. Max 70ch line length.

label.purpose: Section headings inside cards, form field labels, tab bar text. Always uppercase.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Resting state for cards and the navbar. Barely visible — separates surfaces without declaring depth.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Hover state for cards and stat boxes. Shadow intensifies, violet tint becomes perceptible — element feels responsive.

shadows.name: float
shadows.purpose: Active modals and raised panels. The loudest shadow in the system; used sparingly.

shadows.name: glow-primary
shadows.purpose: Resting violet glow on primary buttons. Distinguishes interactive controls from structural cards.

shadows.name: glow-primary-hover
shadows.purpose: Intensified glow on primary button hover.

motion.name: micro
motion.purpose: Button states, tab hover, input focus — immediate feedback interactions.

motion.name: standard
motion.purpose: Card hover lift, stat box elevation — slightly slower for spatial transitions.

motion.name: theme
motion.purpose: Dark mode toggle — smooth surface transition.

motion.name: toast-enter
motion.purpose: Toast notification entrance — standard material easing.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Minimum supported width. All layouts must function at this size.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Two-column form grid activates above this.

breakpoints.name: desktop
breakpoints.purpose: Max content width container. Desktop layout enhancements.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action. Used for save, submit, and primary confirmations.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; padding: 10px 20px; background: #6c4fff; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 0.83rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(108,79,255,0.3); transition: background 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #5538e8; box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(108,79,255,0.4); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(108,79,255,0.2); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(108,79,255,0.35); }

components.name: Success Button
components.description: Positive secondary action. Used for income entry and confirmations that carry a positive signal.
components.css: .ds-btn-success { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; padding: 10px 20px; background: #edfaf3; color: #0a8c5a; border: 0.5px solid #b3e8cc; border-radius: 8px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 0.83rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-success:hover { background: #0a8c5a; color: #ffffff; border-color: #0a8c5a; } .ds-btn-success:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-success:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(10,140,90,0.25); }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive action. Used for delete and remove operations.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; padding: 10px 20px; background: #fff0f0; color: #e74c3c; border: 0.5px solid rgba(231,76,60,0.25); border-radius: 8px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 0.83rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #e74c3c; color: #ffffff; border-color: #e74c3c; } .ds-btn-danger:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(231,76,60,0.25); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Core structural unit. Groups related data on a white surface lifted off the violet-misted page.
components.html:
Recent Expenses

Card content goes here.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 0.5px solid #e8e8f4; border-radius: 14px; box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(108,79,255,0.06); padding: 20px 22px; transition: box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(108,79,255,0.1); } .ds-card-heading { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; color: #9a9ab0; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.8px; margin-bottom: 18px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-card-heading::before { content: ''; width: 3px; height: 12px; background: #6c4fff; border-radius: 2px; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text/number input. Violet-mist background at rest, white with violet glow ring on focus.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-field-label { font-size: 0.65rem; font-weight: 700; color: #9a9ab0; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.7px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-field-input { padding: 10px 13px; background: #f0f0ff; border: 0.5px solid #e8e8f4; border-radius: 8px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 0.88rem; color: #1a1a2e; transition: border-color 0.15s, background 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; width: 100%; } .ds-field-input::placeholder { color: #c0c0d8; } .ds-field-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #6c4fff; background: #ffffff; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(108,79,255,0.12); }

components.name: Tab Bar
components.description: Horizontal tab navigation. 7 tabs for the main app sections. Active tab underlined in violet.
components.css: .ds-tab-bar { display: flex; background: #ffffff; border-bottom: 0.5px solid #e8e8f4; overflow-x: auto; scrollbar-width: none; padding: 0 12px; } .ds-tab-bar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; } .ds-tab-btn { flex: 1; min-width: 72px; height: 46px; padding: 0 14px; border: none; background: none; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 600; color: #9a9ab0; cursor: pointer; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; transition: color 0.15s, background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s; letter-spacing: 0.5px; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-tab-btn:hover { color: #6c4fff; background: #f0eeff; } .ds-tab-btn--active { color: #6c4fff; border-bottom-color: #6c4fff; background: #f0eeff; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pill-shaped status indicator. Variants: online (green), offline (red), pending (amber), default (muted).
components.css: .ds-badge { font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 20px; background: #f4f4fc; color: #9a9ab0; border: 0.5px solid #e8e8f4; letter-spacing: 0.2px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-badge--online { background: #edfaf3; color: #0a8c5a; border-color: #b3e8cc; } .ds-badge--offline { background: #fff0f0; color: #e74c3c; border-color: rgba(231,76,60,0.3); } .ds-badge--pending { background: #fff8e1; color: #f59e0b; border-color: rgba(245,158,11,0.3); }

components.name: Expense List Item
components.description: Transaction row. Emoji icon, title, metadata, and amount. Slides right on hover with violet tint.
components.html:
🍔
Lunch at ChipotleFood · Today
-$14.50
components.css: .ds-expense-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 12px 14px; background: #f0f0ff; border: 0.5px solid #f4f4fc; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .ds-expense-item:hover { background: #f0eeff; border-color: #d4ccff; transform: translateX(2px); } .ds-expense-icon { width: 38px; height: 38px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 1.2rem; background: #ffffff; border-radius: 10px; border: 0.5px solid #e8e8f4; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-expense-meta { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; } .ds-expense-title { font-size: 0.88rem; font-weight: 500; color: #1a1a2e; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-expense-sub { font-size: 0.72rem; color: #9a9ab0; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-expense-amount { font-size: 0.88rem; font-weight: 700; color: #e74c3c; letter-spacing: -0.3px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; }

narrative.overview:
SmartExpense is built for people who don't feel confident about money yet — young professionals watching their first paycheck disappear, students trying to make rent, families deciding what to cut. The interface must feel like a knowledgeable friend: warm enough to not make them feel judged, structured enough to actually help them improve. Every screen earns trust before it earns compliance.

Density is intentional: enough information to be useful in a 30-second check-in, nothing that overwhelms on first visit. Cards group related data without nesting. Purple anchors the experience as a signal of clarity and focus — not luxury, not technicality, just 'this is the thing you pay attention to.' White space is generous; the interface breathes. Progress (health scores, budget bars, trend lines) is always the hero; raw data is secondary.

This system explicitly rejects the corporate gray of enterprise finance tools (Quickbooks, SAP), the data-wall anxiety of Bloomberg and old Mint, and the aggressive neon energy of crypto and trading apps. It also refuses generic bank-app blandness — sterile white with navy headers and zero personality.
keyCharacteristics: Purple-tinted neutrals — even the page background carries a faint violet tint, keeping surfaces warm rather than cold
Progress-first hierarchy — health scores, budget bars, and trend lines are primary; raw data is secondary
State-responsive elevation — shadows appear as feedback to interaction, not decoration at rest
Mobile-first density — everything works at 375px without horizontal scroll or pinch-zoom
Dark mode as first-class — full parallel token set, not an afterthought

rules.name: The Violet Signal Rule
rules.body: Momentum Violet appears on exactly one interactive element per context — the active tab, the focused input, the primary button. Its scarcity is its meaning. If it appears everywhere, it means nothing.

rules.name: The Tinted Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral in this system carries a faint violet undertone. Never use pure #000000, #ffffff, or gray-family (#888888, #cccccc). The tint is what keeps the interface feeling cohesive rather than assembled.

rules.name: The Weight-Contrast Rule
rules.body: At least a 200-unit weight gap between adjacent hierarchy levels. 800 headline into 400 body reads clearly. 600 into 500 does not.

rules.name: The Number-Tightening Rule
rules.body: Negative letter-spacing is reserved for numerical display only (-1px to -1.5px). Prose and labels never use negative tracking.

rules.name: The State-Only Rule
rules.body: Elements do not receive shadow at rest unless they are interactive (cards, buttons, navbar). Static text blocks, dividers, and layout containers are always flat.

rules.name: The Glow-Not-Drop Rule
rules.body: Primary buttons carry a violet ambient glow rather than a neutral drop shadow. The glow intensifies on hover. This distinguishes interactive controls from structural cards.

dos: Do use Momentum Violet (#6c4fff) on one interactive element per context at a time. Its scarcity is its signal.
Do tint every neutral toward violet. Never use pure gray (#888), pure white (#fff), or pure black (#000).
Do pair every color-coded status with a text label or icon shape. Never rely on color alone.
Do keep body line length under 70ch. Financial information reads fastest in a narrower column.
Do apply transform: scale(0.97) on button :active — it makes interactions feel physical.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion — suppress translateY lifts and reduce transition durations.
Do let cards breathe at 20px+ internal padding. Compressed cards feel like the corporate tools this product is not.
donts: Don't use Quickbooks/SAP visual language — no corporate gray as primary surfaces, no dense form grids with 10+ fields visible at once.
Don't build data walls. One primary insight per screen surface.
Don't use aggressive crypto or trading aesthetics — no neon on dark, no price-ticker animations.
Don't ship generic bank-app blandness — sterile white with a navy header and zero personality.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a standalone colored stripe accent on list items or callouts.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient fill). Emphasis belongs to weight and size.
Don't animate layout properties (width, height, top, left, margin). Use transform and opacity only.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing. Ease-out-quart or expo for all transitions.
Don't reach for a modal as the first solution for any new interaction.
